input.background: Exercises 16.2 and 16.4 considered a problem in which seven of eight patients expressed a preference for medical procedure A compared to medical procedure B. A Normal approximation test was precluded because of the small sample size.
input.question: Calculate the one-sided $P$-value for the test.

output.answer: $P$ (one-sided) $=\operatorname{Pr}(X=7)+\operatorname{Pr}(X=8)=0.0313+0.0039=$ 0.0352. This provided good evidence against the null hypothesis.
